channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Taking Notes to Forget — The Idea of Cognitive Offloading (Bite-size Article)
item.description: Introduction Today I want to write about note-taking. I've always had this sense that I take notes in order to forget things. Rather than trying to retain information, I prefer to just move it...

item.title: Building a Personalized Code Learning Platform with LangChain 0.2 and Next.js 15
item.description: 87% of developers abandon self-paced learning platforms within 14 days due to generic, one-size-fits-all content, according to a 2025 Stack Overflow survey. After 15 years building developer tools...

item.title: Understanding Transformers Part 17: Generating the Output Word
item.description: In the previous article, we set up the residual connections to get the final output values from the decoder. In this article, we begin by passing these two output values through a fully connected...

item.title: I built a self-hosted AI agent that markets itself. Here's how.
item.description: Two months ago my co-founder and I got tired of paying for AI tools that live on someone else's server, phone home constantly, and charge per seat. So we built our own. Today we're open sourcing...

item.title: Step-by-Step: Self-Host Matrix 2.0 with Docker 27 and PostgreSQL 17
item.description: Step-by-Step: Self-Host Matrix 2.0 with Docker 27 and PostgreSQL 17 Matrix 2.0 is the latest iteration of the open, decentralized communication protocol, bringing improved performance, native...

item.title: Identity on Blockchain: How It Differs from the Traditional Web (with a Focus on Solana)
item.description: Over the past week, I started learning about blockchain, and one of the first concepts I encountered was identity. In our everyday lives, identity is the way we prove who we are. Governments issue...

item.title: Why CLI over MCP?
item.description: Everyone wants to make things agent-friendly now, so are MCPs better than CLIs? When Claude Code started hitting limits, I began using Kimi (with OpenCode) alongside Claude. Only then, I realized...

item.title: The O(n^2) Bug That Looked Like Clean Code
item.description: We shipped a feature on a Tuesday. By Thursday the API was timing out. The monitoring dashboard showed p99 latency climbing from 80ms to 14 seconds over 48 hours. Nothing had changed except the...

item.title: Credit Cards Are Vulnerable to Brute Force Kind Attacks
item.description: Article URL: https://metin.nextc.org/posts/Credit_Cards_Are_Vulnerable_To_Brute_Force_Kind_Attacks.html Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47979839 Points: 9 # Comments: 4

item.title: A Dark-Money Campaign Is Paying Influencers to Frame Chinese AI as a Threat
item.description: Build American AI, a nonprofit linked to a super PAC bankrolled by executives at OpenAI and Andreessen Horowitz, is funding a campaign to spread pro-AI messaging and stoke fears about China.

item.title: 3 Asyncio Pitfalls That Took Me 3 Hours to Debug and Almost Crashed Production
item.description: Here’s the story: last week my lead asked me to optimize a data aggregation service that calls 20 downstream APIs. The serial version took around 18 seconds — users were ready to throw their...

item.title: All the evidence revealed so far in Musk v. Altman
item.description: The Musk v. Altman trial is underway, and that means exhibits, or the evidence to be presented in court, are being revealed piece by piece. So far, email exchanges, photos, and corporate documents...

item.title: Ubuntu infrastructure has been down for more than a day
item.description: The outage has hampered communication concerning a critical vulnerability that gives root.

item.title: Birdfy’s smart bird feeder is down to its best-ever price for Mother’s Day
item.description: From crafting machines to smart calendars, a number of unique gifts are already on sale in the run-up to Mother’s Day, May 10th. Birdfy smart feeders are another discounted option to consider,...

item.title: 30,000 Facebook Accounts Hacked via Google AppSheet Phishing Campaign
item.description: A newly discovered Vietnamese-linked operation has been observed using a Google AppSheet as a "phishing relay" to distribute phishing emails with an aim to compromise Facebook accounts. The...

item.title: Amazon’s built-in AI price history expands to show the entire last year
item.description: Amazon's built-in price tracking feature now allows you to see how much a product's price has changed over the past year. To use the feature, open the Amazon app and select the "Price history"...

item.title: Dreame — the vacuum company — just ‘launched’ its own phones
item.description: Dreame, a Chinese manufacturer best known for its robot vacuums but with ambitions to do much more, says it's making smartphones now. I'm not sure I believe it. The company showed off two phones...

item.title: Did you know you can’t steal a charity? Don’t worry. Elon Musk will remind you.
item.description: Elon Musk spent the better part of three days on the witness stand this week in his lawsuit against OpenAI, and it’s already getting messy. Emails, texts, and his own tweets are surfacing in...

item.title: This accessory can snap a Steam Controller to your phone — or almost anything else
item.description: Valve's new Steam Controller goes on sale on Monday for $99, and accessories-maker Mechanism will be ready. As far as we know, Mechanism's new Basegrip is the very first way to attach a Steam...

item.title: Players from the NBA, NFL, and MLB call for a ban on betting ‘unders’
item.description: The unions backing professional NBA, NFL, MLB, NHL, and MLS players are calling on the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) to ban prediction market platforms from allowing users to bet on...

item.title: Severe Linux Copy Fail security flaw uncovered using AI scanning help
item.description: Nearly every Linux distribution released since 2017 is currently vulnerable to a security bug called "Copy Fail" that allows any user to give themselves administrator privileges. The exploit,...

item.title: Dreame’s rocket-powered car can do 0–60 in 0.9 seconds because you can just say things now
item.description: When I first heard that a vacuum company had released a rocket-powered electric vehicle with physically impossible-sounding performance specs, I immediately thought James Dyson was up to his old...

item.title: We just got a new reason to believe the Trump phone is real(ish)
item.description: Where's the Trump phone? We're going to keep talking about it every week. We've reached out, as usual, to ask about the Trump phone's whereabouts. This time we've discovered a little more evidence...

item.title: Pentagon inks deals with Nvidia, Microsoft, and AWS to deploy AI on classified networks
item.description: The deals come as the DOD has doubled down on diversifying its exposure to AI vendors in the wake of its controversial dispute with Anthropic over usage terms of its AI models.

item.title: Cyber-Insecurity in the AI Era
item.description: Cybersecurity was already under strain before AI entered the stack. Now, as AI expands the attack surface and adds new complexity, the limits of legacy approaches are becoming harder to ignore....

item.title: Operationalizing AI for Scale and Sovereignty
item.description: Companies are taking control of their own data to tailor AI for their needs. The challenge lies in balancing ownership with the safe, trusted flow of high‑quality data needed to power reliable...

item.title: Cybercrime Groups Using Vishing and SSO Abuse in Rapid SaaS Extortion Attacks
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers are warning of two cybercrime groups that are carrying out "rapid, high-impact attacks" operating almost within the confines of SaaS environments, while leaving minimal...

item.title: China-Linked Hackers Target Asian Governments, NATO State, Journalists, and Activists
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new China-aligned espionage campaign targeting government and defense sectors across South, East, and Southeast Asia, along with one European...

item.title: Musk v. Altman is just getting started
item.description: Elon Musk spent the better part of three days on the witness stand this week in his lawsuit against OpenAI, and it’s already getting messy. Emails, texts, and his own tweets are surfacing in...

item.title: The Download: a new Christian phone network, and debugging LLMs
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. A new US phone network for Christians aims to block porn...
